questions.main_text:
You are taking a course on creative writing and are asked to discuss your thoughts and feelings after reading this poem by e.e.cummings. Please write your reflections in the form of an essay.

​

​

Me up at does

​

out of the floor

quietly Stare

​

a poisoned mouse

​

still who alive

​

is asking What

have i done that

​

You wouldn’t have

content.text:
You are taking a course on creative writing and are asked to discuss your thoughts and feelings after reading this poem by e.e.cummings. Please write your reflections in the form of an essay.

​

​

Me up at does

​

out of the floor

quietly Stare

​

a poisoned mouse

​

still who alive

​

is asking What

have i done that

​

You wouldn’t have

questions.main_text:
**Blog postcard**

​

You are spending a semester abroad as an exchange student, and the organisation arranging the exchange requires that all exchange students send a “blog postcard” to the organisation’s blog. In the blog postcard, the students should briefly describe their studies there and the target culture in general and compare them with their home country. Write your blog postcard.

content.text:
**Blog postcard**

​

You are spending a semester abroad as an exchange student, and the organisation arranging the exchange requires that all exchange students send a “blog postcard” to the organisation’s blog. In the blog postcard, the students should briefly describe their studies there and the target culture in general and compare them with their home country. Write your blog postcard.

questions.main_text:
I firstly realised that owning a car in a city is slightly demented when a friend in Manhattan [SELECT id=178] me her Upper West Side apartment for a month. When I arrived, I discovered the catch for her seeming generosity – the keys to her Jeep Cherokee and a breezy request to “look after it”, [SELECT id=179] with “Please move it for street cleaning after five pm today”.

​

It took two parking tickets in week one to learn that park [SELECT id=180], certainly in New York, is way more onerous than pet 3)____________ . Ticket one was for not moving it; number two, seven days later, for moving it – to a lovely but forbidden spot by a fire hydrant.

​

The endlessly tedious West 76th Street experience [SELECT id=181] me to sell my old Saab before heading to the US for a few months last year.

​

Even though you’re legally parked in my borough, the council can move your car whenever they fancy it, charge £200 [SELECT id=182] the helpful tow-away, and then £40-a-day storage. But now I’m back for a while at what is nominally home, I thought I’d buy another car. [SELECT id=183] I haven’t, and I’m not sure I will.

​

Not owning a car is also giving me a feeling, not wholly delusional, that I’m doing something to make cities better, and to put cars to more sensible use. I’m no great friend of the Earth, but is there [SELECT id=184] dumber, really, than good machines [SELECT id=185] idle ninety-five per cent of the time?

​

I touched last week on that idea that disruption is overstated, overrated and overhyped. A commenter, TKR, [SELECT id=186], however, that what disruptive tech does marvellously is exploit unemployed resources like our chronically underused cars.

content.text:
I firstly realised that owning a car in a city is slightly demented when a friend in Manhattan [SELECT id=178] me her Upper West Side apartment for a month. When I arrived, I discovered the catch for her seeming generosity – the keys to her Jeep Cherokee and a breezy request to “look after it”, [SELECT id=179] with “Please move it for street cleaning after five pm today”.

​

It took two parking tickets in week one to learn that park [SELECT id=180], certainly in New York, is way more onerous than pet 3)____________ . Ticket one was for not moving it; number two, seven days later, for moving it – to a lovely but forbidden spot by a fire hydrant.

​

The endlessly tedious West 76th Street experience [SELECT id=181] me to sell my old Saab before heading to the US for a few months last year.

​

Even though you’re legally parked in my borough, the council can move your car whenever they fancy it, charge £200 [SELECT id=182] the helpful tow-away, and then £40-a-day storage. But now I’m back for a while at what is nominally home, I thought I’d buy another car. [SELECT id=183] I haven’t, and I’m not sure I will.

​

Not owning a car is also giving me a feeling, not wholly delusional, that I’m doing something to make cities better, and to put cars to more sensible use. I’m no great friend of the Earth, but is there [SELECT id=184] dumber, really, than good machines [SELECT id=185] idle ninety-five per cent of the time?

​

I touched last week on that idea that disruption is overstated, overrated and overhyped. A commenter, TKR, [SELECT id=186], however, that what disruptive tech does marvellously is exploit unemployed resources like our chronically underused cars.

questions.main_text:
Koe koostuu neljästä osasta: kuullunymmärtämistehtävät, tekstin­ymmärtämistehtävät, sanasto- ja rakennetehtävät ja kirjallinen tuottaminen.

​

Voit tehdä tehtävät haluamassasi järjestyksessä. Noudata tehtäväkohtaisia vastaamisohjeita. Voit muuttaa antamiasi vastauksia koska tahansa kokeen aikana. Tee kukin tehtävä loppuun ennen kuin aloitat seuraavan.

​

Vastaa monivalintatehtäviin ja monivalinta-aukkotehtäviin napsauttamalla kulloinkin sopivinta vaihtoehtoa. Kirjoita avoimissa tehtävissä vastauksesi tehtävän yhteydessä olevaan vastauskenttään. Tehtävissä voi olla myös tehtäväkohtaista ohjeistusta.

​

Lisää kielikokeen vastausohjeita on linkissä [LINK url=https://yle.fi/plus/abitreenit/2018/syksy/EA-fi/attachments/Kielikokeen-ohjeistus.pdf|text=Kielikokeen ohjeistus].

​

Kuullunymmärtämistehtävien äänitteet ja videot saat käynnistää omaan tahtiisi napsauttamalla siihen liittyvää painiketta. Kun olet käyttänyt sallitut kuuntelukerrat, et voi enää kuunnella äänitettä uudelleen. Käytetyt painikkeet tunnistaa niiden muuttuneesta väristä. Varaa aikaa kuullun­ymmärtämistehtävien tekemiseen yhteensä noin 1 tunti.

​

Videot katsotaan aineistovälilehdellä olevalla mediasoittimella. Videoita voit katsoa niin monta kertaa kuin haluat. Voit myös pysäyttää videoiden toiston ja kelata niitä eteen- tai taaksepäin.

​

Joissakin tehtävissä on aineistoa, joka löytyy erilliseltä välilehdeltä. Sinne pääset [LINK url=https://yle.fi/plus/abitreenit/2018/syksy/EA-fi/attachments/index.html|text=tästä linkistä]. Yksittäisen tehtävän aineistoon pääset myös suoraan napsauttamalla kyseisen tehtävän yhteydessä olevaa Avaa aineisto -linkkiä.

​

Onnea kokeeseen!

​

Kun olet käyttänyt sallitut kuuntelukerrat, et voi enää kuunnella äänitettä uudelleen. Käytetyt painikkeet tunnistaa niiden muuttuneesta väristä. Varaa aikaa kuullun­ymmärtämistehtävien tekemiseen yhteensä noin 1 tunti.

​

Videot katsotaan aineistovälilehdellä olevalla mediasoittimella. Videoita voit katsoa niin monta kertaa kuin haluat. Voit myös pysäyttää videoiden toiston ja kelata niitä eteen- tai taaksepäin.

​

Joissakin tehtävissä on aineistoa, joka löytyy erilliseltä välilehdeltä. Sinne pääset [LINK url=https://yle.fi/plus/abitreenit/2018/syksy/EA-fi/attachments/index.html|text=tästä linkistä]. Yksittäisen tehtävän aineistoon pääset myös suoraan napsauttamalla kyseisen tehtävän yhteydessä olevaa Avaa aineisto -linkkiä.

​

questions.main_text:
**Private Cloud**

​

Read the text and answer the question in Finnish.

​

Enterprise firms are embracing data analytics to improve both internal processes and the customer’s total experience. Global retailer Walmart is building the world’s biggest private cloud, which will crunch more than 2.5 petabytes of transactions at both retail stores and through the website per hour. For example, the data analysts can judge if an item at the store is underperforming.

content.text:
**Private Cloud**

​

Read the text and answer the question in Finnish.

​

Enterprise firms are embracing data analytics to improve both internal processes and the customer’s total experience. Global retailer Walmart is building the world’s biggest private cloud, which will crunch more than 2.5 petabytes of transactions at both retail stores and through the website per hour. For example, the data analysts can judge if an item at the store is underperforming.

questions.main_text:
**Writer’s Digest**

​

_Writer’s Digest_ magazine has its annual writing contest for young adults. The theme this year is reinvention, and the task is to write about an incident in your past that you would like to have a chance to relive and do differently. Write your entry in the contest.

content.text:
**Writer’s Digest**

​

_Writer’s Digest_ magazine has its annual writing contest for young adults. The theme this year is reinvention, and the task is to write about an incident in your past that you would like to have a chance to relive and do differently. Write your entry in the contest.

questions.main_text: What characterizes the mountain states in the West, such as Idaho and Montana? (3 p.)

content.text: What characterizes the mountain states in the West, such as Idaho and Montana? (3 p.)

options.text: They are not keen on environmentally friendly options
They suffer from there not being much industry
Their demand for energy does not meet the supply

questions.main_text: How has the study been helpful? (2 p.)

content.text: How has the study been helpful? (2 p.)

options.text: It has drawn on a significant number of research sources
It has solved a substantial number of public health problems
It has generated a considerable amount of valuable data

questions.main_text: What is meant by the term “policy” here? (2 p.)

content.text: What is meant by the term “policy” here? (2 p.)

options.text: The interrelationship between various parties
The way one comes across in social situations
The resolutions made in order to manage society

questions.main_text: What best describes Kim’s approach? (2 p.)

content.text: What best describes Kim’s approach? (2 p.)

options.text: She criticises the way the study was conducted
She questions the usefulness of the study results
She comes across as ignorant of earlier studies

questions.main_text: What does the woman say next? (2 p.)

content.text: What does the woman say next? (2 p.)

options.text: A) So thanks, but not for me.
B) So I’ll pass, thanks.
C) So I’m fine, thanks.
D) So thank you, but no.

questions.main_text: Your family members wonder if they could find relief for their problems by taking this medicine. Which one could find help from this medicine? (2 p.)

content.text: Your family members wonder if they could find relief for their problems by taking this medicine. Which one could find help from this medicine? (2 p.)

options.text: Your cousin has bulimia and has developed a urinary tract infection.
Your sister has an upset tummy. She is pregnant.
Your grandfather has trouble sleeping. He is allergic to birch pollen.
Your younger brother has a rash. He is vegan and does not use any animal products.
